title: Selinum gummiferum
taxonomy: Selinum gummiferum is classified in the kingdom Plantae, specifically within the family Apiaceae, which is commonly known as the carrot or parsley family. This family is further divided into the subfamily Apioideae. Selinum gummiferum is a species of flowering plant that belongs to the genus Selinum. The genus Selinum is a group of plants that are closely related to the carrot and parsley. Selinum gummiferum's classification is further detailed within the APG III system.
uses: Selinum gummiferum is a plant species that has been traditionally used in medicine, particularly in Eastern Europe and Asia. Its roots are used to prepare a medicinal infusion, which is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is thought to be effective in treating various health issues, including digestive problems, respiratory infections, and skin conditions. However, more research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of its medicinal uses. Traditional medicine practitioners often recommend Selinum gummiferum as a natural remedy for soothing digestive issues and relieving pain.

intro: Selinum gummiferum, also known as Queen's crown or Queen's lace, is a perennial plant in the carrot family (Apiaceae) native to Europe and Western Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, making it beneficial for treating various health issues such as digestive problems, fever, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Selinum gummiferum include volatile oils, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Queen's crown is often used to make infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to treat coughs, colds, and respiratory issues, as well as to promote digestion and alleviate menstrual cramps.
